title: Papi (3/7/20 — Dad leaves for rehab)
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir at age ~17 writes about Dad leaving for rehab — bilingual entry (English + Spanish translation).
  • Frames Dad as the inspiration-figure of childhood: wrestling, basketball, chess, soccer, basta — "the goal of rising above your greatest inspiration of your dad."
  • Names Dad's addiction as "a snake eating its own tail" — earliest cyclical time image in the dad-arc corpus, predates the 2023 birthday poem mirror-cycle line.
  • Tone is dissociative: "I'm not sad, I'm not mad, I'm not even anxious. The feeling just doesn't really feel like anything." — early numbness as strength datapoint.
  • "I feel hope for the cycle to unleash" — first time hope is named in the dad-arc; predates the 2024 collapse.

Notes

This is the earliest dated entry in the dad-arc corpus that explicitly names rehab as the intervention, written the day Dad departs. The texture is unmistakably Yahir-at-17: oscillating between gratitude ("I truly feel fortunate") and the snake-eating-tail metaphor, then immediately translating the whole thing into Spanish — likely so Mom / family in Mexico could read it.

The wrestling-with-Dad imagery here gets reused in the 2024 "cut from his cloth" doc, where the inheritance-frame goes from inspiration to dread. This 2020 entry is the before of that arc — Dad as model-to-surpass, not yet as warning.

The bilingual register is significant: Yahir defaults to English for thinking, Spanish for family-record. Same dual-channel he uses in Mi Engano.

Connections

  • dad yahir · mom yahir · yahir family · yermy
  • alcoholism in the family · lineage as fate · numbness as strength
